title: fab doctor — two-layer byte-level drift detection (L1 canonical ↔ snapshot, L2 snapshot+rules ↔ three-end blocks)
description: Add two new doctor inspections + matching fix paths: (L1) `bootstrap_snapshot_drift` = byte-compare BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL ↔ .fabric/AGENTS.md; (L2) `managed_block_drift` = byte-compare `buildExpectedBlockBody(target)` (= .fabric/AGENTS.md + optional `\n---\n` + .fabric/project-rules.md) ↔ each three-end managed block extracted via BOOTSTRAP_REGEX. Drift → fixable_error (report-only abort exit=1); `fab doctor --fix` overwrites with canonical/concat content. NO normalization (CRLF differences trigger drift — install bug surfacing). Mirrors existing inspectBootstrapAnchor / createBootstrapAnchorCheck / fixX trio at doctor.ts:1779.

files.change: Append '.fabric/AGENTS.md' and '.fabric/project-rules.md' so summary.targetFiles surfaces their presence in --json output.

Add `inspectL1BootstrapSnapshotDrift(target): Promise<{ status: 'ok' | 'missing' | 'drift'; canonical: string; onDisk: string | null }>` — reads .fabric/AGENTS.md, byte-compares against BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL from @fenglimg/fabric-shared; returns 'missing' if file absent (delegated to existing bootstrap_anchor_missing), 'drift' if bytes differ. Add `createL1BootstrapSnapshotDriftCheck(inspection)` — fixable_error code 'bootstrap_snapshot_drift' when status='drift', actionHint='Run `fab doctor --fix` to restore canonical bootstrap snapshot'.

Add `inspectL2ManagedBlockDrift(target): Promise<{ status: 'ok' | 'drift' | 'no-managed-block'; drifted: Array<{ path: string; expected: string; actual: string }> }>` — computes `expectedBody = readFileSync('.fabric/AGENTS.md') + (project-rules.md? '\n---\n' + readFileSync(...) : '')`; for each of [root AGENTS.md, .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc]: extract managed block body via BOOTSTRAP_REGEX, byte-compare against expectedBody; for CLAUDE.md verify `@.fabric/AGENTS.md` line present (no managed block in Claude thin shell). Returns drift list with paths + expected/actual. Skip files where new marker is absent BUT legacy marker is present (TASK-04 marker_migration_required handles those first). Add `createL2ManagedBlockDriftCheck(inspection)` — fixable_error code 'managed_block_drift' with drifted paths in message, actionHint='Run `fab doctor --fix` to restore three-end managed blocks from canonical'.

Append two new createXCheck calls AFTER the existing bootstrap_anchor_missing check (preserves order: anchor existence → migration → L1 → L2). The two new inspections live in the Promise.all block at L708-L718.

Add two new if-blocks AFTER the marker migration block from TASK-04: (1) `if (before.fixable_errors.some(i => i.code === 'bootstrap_snapshot_drift')) { await atomicWriteText(join(projectRoot, '.fabric/AGENTS.md'), BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL); fixed.push(findIssue(before, 'bootstrap_snapshot_drift')!); }`, (2) `if (before.fixable_errors.some(i => i.code === 'managed_block_drift')) { await rewriteThreeEndManagedBlocks(projectRoot); fixed.push(findIssue(before, 'managed_block_drift')!); }`. Order is critical: L1 fix MUST run before L2 fix (L2 depends on canonical snapshot being correct).

Add `async function rewriteThreeEndManagedBlocks(projectRoot)` that re-applies the three install writers (writeClaudeBootstrapThinShell / writeCodexBootstrapManagedBlock / writeCursorBootstrapManagedBlock from TASK-03). BUT: doctor lives in packages/server which cannot import from packages/cli. So either (a) inline a minimal in-place block rewrite using BOOTSTRAP_REGEX + buildManagedBlockBody (recommended — small, no new dep) OR (b) hoist the three writers into packages/shared. Choice: (a) — duplicate the minimal regex+replace logic in doctor.ts (~40 LOC) to keep cross-package boundary clean. Helper reads expectedBody, then for each of [AGENTS.md, .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc]: read existing file, replace BOOTSTRAP_REGEX with `{BOOTSTRAP_MARKER_BEGIN}\n{expectedBody}\n{BOOTSTRAP_MARKER_END}`, atomicWriteText. For CLAUDE.md: ensure `@.fabric/AGENTS.md` line present (idempotent line-add) + optional `@.fabric/project-rules.md` line if file exists.

implementation: Implement inspectL1BootstrapSnapshotDrift — byte-compare canonical vs disk; status union 'ok'|'missing'|'drift'

Implement inspectL2ManagedBlockDrift — extract managed blocks via BOOTSTRAP_REGEX from AGENTS.md + .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc; verify @import lines in CLAUDE.md; byte-compare against expectedBody
Implement createL2ManagedBlockDriftCheck — fixable_error with code 'managed_block_drift'; message includes drifted file paths
Wire both new checks into runDoctorReport's checks aggregation AFTER bootstrap_anchor_missing check
Add L1 and L2 if-blocks to runDoctorFix dispatcher AFTER the marker migration block (TASK-04) — order critical
Implement rewriteThreeEndManagedBlocks helper in doctor.ts (NOT cross-package import) — minimal regex+replace inline
Verify NO normalization layer added — content read as raw utf8 with default encoding; CRLF differences MUST trigger drift
Verify happy path: post-install with no manual edits, runDoctorReport returns status='ok'

reference.pattern: Existing inspectBootstrapAnchor / createBootstrapAnchorCheck trio at doctor.ts:1779. Existing fixMcpConfigInWrongFile (L4395) for the file-rewrite pattern.
reference.examples: issueCheck signature: (name, status, kind, code, message, actionHint). Set actionHint to 'Run `fab doctor --fix` to ...' for consistency with existing actionHints.

criteria: Happy path (post-install, no edits): runDoctorReport returns status='ok'; fixable_errors[] contains neither 'bootstrap_snapshot_drift' nor 'managed_block_drift'
L1 drift seed (edit .fabric/AGENTS.md by one char): runDoctorReport returns status='error' with code='bootstrap_snapshot_drift' in fixable_errors
After runDoctorFix on L1 drift: .fabric/AGENTS.md byte-equals BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL; re-run report returns status='ok'
L2 drift seed (edit managed block in AGENTS.md): report returns code='managed_block_drift' in fixable_errors
After runDoctorFix on L2 drift: managed block bytes-equal expectedBody (snapshot + optional project-rules concat); re-run report returns status='ok'
CRLF drift seed (rewrite .fabric/AGENTS.md with \r\n line endings): drift fires (NO normalization layer)
Idempotency: second runDoctorFix on already-fixed state produces zero file rewrites and zero new ledger events
L1 fix runs BEFORE L2 fix in dispatcher (verify by reading runDoctorFix top-down)
Doctor without --fix on drift state exits with process.exitCode=1 (report-only abort)
Cross-package boundary respected: packages/server has NO new import from packages/cli (all canonical/marker imports come from @fenglimg/fabric-shared)

rationale.chosen_approach: Two separate inspections + two separate fixable_error codes for explicit operator visibility (L1 vs L2 drift have different root causes). L1 fix rewrites canonical only; L2 fix replays managed-block writes from canonical+project-rules concat. Order: marker_migration → L1 → L2 (each layer depends on previous).
alternatives_considered: Hoist install writers to shared and import from doctor (creates a runtime dep from server to cli's write paths)
rationale.tradeoffs: ~40 LOC duplication of managed-block-write logic in doctor vs cross-package import. Acceptable; logic is simple regex replace and the duplication is the explicit cost of the architectural boundary.

manual_checks: Run install on tmpdir fixture, verify doctor reports ok
Edit .fabric/AGENTS.md (one char), run doctor, verify L1 drift detected
Run doctor --fix, verify .fabric/AGENTS.md restored, doctor reports ok
Edit managed block in root AGENTS.md, verify L2 drift detected
Run doctor --fix, verify managed block restored
Re-write .fabric/AGENTS.md with CRLF endings, verify L1 drift fires (no normalization)
success_metrics: Drift detection round-trip works for all three managed targets
integration: Covered by TASK-09 doctor service-layer drift tests

risks.description: Byte-compare must NOT normalize line endings — accidentally adding a .replace(/\r\n/g, '\n') in inspect helper would hide install-side CRLF bugs
risks.mitigation: Test fixture explicitly writes CRLF content and asserts drift fires (regression guard); code review checklist item

risks.description: rewriteThreeEndManagedBlocks duplication may drift from install-side writers if install changes
risks.mitigation: Comment in both locations cross-referencing the other; integration test verifies post-doctor-fix and post-install states are byte-equal

risks.description: L2 inspection on a file with legacy marker (pre-migration) — should NOT report L2 drift; should defer to TASK-04 migration
risks.mitigation: L2 inspect helper checks BOOTSTRAP_REGEX (new marker) presence first; if absent AND LEGACY_KB_REGEX present, returns status='no-managed-block' (which is ok) — TASK-04 already flagged the file via marker_migration_required

key_functions.signature: inspectL1BootstrapSnapshotDrift(target: string): Promise<{ status: 'ok' | 'missing' | 'drift'; canonical: string; onDisk: string | null }>

key_functions.purpose: Build fixable_error check from L1 inspection

key_functions.signature: inspectL2ManagedBlockDrift(target: string): Promise<{ status: 'ok' | 'drift' | 'no-managed-block'; drifted: Array<{path, expected, actual}> }>
key_functions.purpose: Byte-compare managed block bodies against expectedBody (snapshot + optional concat)
Build fixable_error check from L2 inspection
Replay three-end managed-block writes using minimal inline regex+replace logic
